assumptions: The dividend receivable is a recognised intragroup monetary item denominated in a currency different from the relevant functional currency used for the consolidated hedge designation.
The related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation and can affect consolidated profit or loss.
The entity has an eligible hedging instrument and can satisfy the designation, documentation and effectiveness requirements in IFRS 9 paragraph 6.4.1.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised foreign-currency receivable can be analysed under the fair value hedge model because IFRS 9 allows a recognised asset or liability exposed to a particular risk affecting profit or loss to be a hedged item.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised foreign-currency receivable can also be analysed under the cash flow hedge model if exchange-rate movements create variability in cash flows that could affect profit or loss.
